00:13:41	danceratopz:	🏗️ EIP-8282 builder deposit contract (devnet-7 + audit) [optional]
https://github.com/ethereum/sys-asm/pull/43
https://github.com/ethereum/sys-asm/pull/48
Per-tx fee merged; open: pass target/max/rate via calldata + final constants, then freeze for the audit.

00:14:35	Barnabas:	btw nimbus still doesn’t seem to use builder’s to build blocks
00:17:07	Barnabas:	Should we do a new contract if we wanna tweak the numbers?
00:17:24	Barnabas:	Feels like a lot more disposable contract vs the validator contract tbh
00:18:50	Felix (Geth):	Regarding 'disposable contract', one big thing with 8282 is that the contract stores the actual deposits
00:19:28	Felix (Geth):	So if we would launch another contract later, the builders might deposit to the old contract that we are not polling anymore, and their deposit would be lost forever
00:19:43	Barnabas:	Replying to "Regarding 'disposabl..."

right but when builders would just exit, it would create the eth from beacon chain no?
00:20:02	Justin Florentine (Besu):	Replying to "So if we would launc..."

makes sense to decouple the queue from the depositing account, but it will complicate and lengthen the audit
00:20:03	Felix (Geth):	If we want to upgrade to another contract later, then I think we should add a logic into the contract that disables it
00:20:35	Barnabas:	self destruct? (joke btw)
00:21:00	Nico Flaig:	target=8,max=64 instead of target=32, max=256 on the builder deposit contract, that's all my change does, we can then reduce withdrawal delay for builders on the CL side further without opening up a dos of the validator withdrawal sweep
00:21:44	Justin Traglia:	Reacted to "target=8,max=64 inst..." with 👍
00:24:25	Toni Wahrstätter:	lowering the target should make it cheaper no?
00:24:41	Felix (Geth):	here is my PR for reading params from system call: https://github.com/ethereum/sys-asm/pull/48
00:25:05	pk910:	I get that the dynamic fee might be too complex.   but I thinks it's very bad to change the public contract address at a later fork.
Disabling deposits on the old contract helps a bit,  but still breaks downstream toolings and needs dev work.
Could we make the depsoit contract a proxy where we can change the implementation via a system call? :D (kinda joking as it's likely more complex,  but would solve it too)
00:25:08	potuz:	Replying to "lowering the target ..."

I think lowering it should make it more expensive
00:25:26	Felix (Geth):	proxy is ok too
00:26:36	pk910:	Announcing a deposit contract address once is something else than changing the contract address later imo
00:27:42	potuz:	We don’t expect to have builder churn on that contract, we shouldn’t spend too much time on this, deploying new contracts is absolutely fine
00:28:35	Stefan Starflinger:	Reacted to "Announcing a deposit..." with 👍
00:29:00	Felix (Geth):	we can also do a thing where we pull from both contracts in the fork N -> N+1 window
00:29:18	Felix (Geth):	and then stop pulling from it at fork N+1
00:30:35	Toni Wahrstätter:	Let's keep it with using constants (instead of dynamic) but try avoid needing a new contract by chosing the constants well
